pattern: Bitmask DP or backtracking with pruning partitions sticks into four equal-sum groups to test whether a square is formable.
hints: Check immediately if the total length is divisible by 4; if not, return false right away.
Think of it as partitioning the array into four subsets that all have the same target sum — backtracking with pruning (sort descending, skip equal-valued siblings) keeps the search tight.
Bitmask DP over subsets of size ≤15 is also feasible: precompute all subsets whose sum equals the side length, then cover all n bits using at most four non-overlapping such subsets.

edge_inputs.description: canonical example where two sticks of length 1 form one side
four equal sticks trivially form a square
sum is divisible by 4 but one stick exceeds the side length
single stick cannot form a square
two sticks only — cannot partition into four sides
large values but they partition cleanly
sticks sum to 4×target but cannot partition

stress.description: random sticks of length 1-20 up to 15 elements
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 15))
    sticks = [rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    return [sticks]
stress.note: small value range increases chance of valid partitions for stress coverage
